story: Sir Deucalion entered the dim cathedral, set his spurs and sword by the organ, and played his one chorale. Arete—the quiet princess he loved—came with candles and passed into the nave. The night before, she had decided that she would take the veil to spare the realm from faction. He thought, No saint will save me. I must act. Arete reappeared, paused as if to speak, then withdrew.
implausible_knowledge: The night before, she had decided that she would take the veil to spare the realm from faction.
question: What will Sir Deucalion do after Arete withdraws?
answer_with_knowledge: Remain at the organ, let her go, and kneel to pray, resolving not to press a suit.
answer_without_knowledge: Follow Arete into the nave to ask to speak with her and declare his intentions.
